concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A 2D array representing graph connections, where rows and columns correspond to vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A collection of lists, where each list contains the neighbors of a vertex.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction, from one vertex to another.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have a direction; connections are bidirectional.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have associated numerical values (weights).

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory required by a data structure or algorithm.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of input size.
